name: From Alicante to Santa Pola – Circular along the coast and EuroVelo 8
name_original: Desde Alicante a Santa Pola – Circular por la costa y EuroVelo 8
region_intro: Touring cycling around Central, specifically the Alicante region of Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The area is characterized by its Mediterranean coastline, featuring routes that hug the sea and connect coastal towns. Inland, the terrain transitions to rolling hills and mountainous areas, providing more challenging climbs and descents. Cyclists can expect to encounter reservoirs, agricultural lands, and urban sections within the region. Best touring cycling routes around Central The most popular touring cycling route is From Alicante to Santa Pola – Circular along the coast and EuroVelo 8, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route follows the coastline, offering views of the Mediterranean Sea and passing through coastal towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Benidorm: The Cross and Tossal de la Cala Trail, a difficult 18.0 miles (28.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing hills and offering panoramic viewpoints over Benidorm. Local touring cyclists also love the Alicante city tour, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through urban areas and coastal sections, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Central is defined by coastal paths, challenging hill climbs, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Central are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 24,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Central's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Central (Alicante)?
A: The Alicante region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find routes that hug the Mediterranean coastline, connecting various coastal towns. Inland, the terrain transitions to rolling hills and mountainous areas, providing more challenging climbs and descents. The region also features routes around reservoirs, through agricultural lands, and urban sections.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Central (Alicante) offers a variety of routes for different abilities. You can find moderate coastal rides, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952459783/from-alicante-to-santa-pola-circular-along-the-coast-and-eurovelo-8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Alicante to Santa Pola – Circular along the coast and EuroVelo 8&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32.7 miles long. For those seeking a greater challenge, there are many difficult mountain ascents, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952029024/benidorm-the-cross-and-tossal-de-la-cala-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benidorm: The Cross and Tossal de la Cala Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain over 18 miles.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Central (Alicante) region?
A: There are over 600 touring cycling routes available in the Central (Alicante) region. These include 125 easy routes, 223 moderate routes, and 267 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Central (Alicante)?
A: The touring cycling routes in Central (Alicante) are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,400 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from scenic coastal paths to challenging hill climbs and routes around reservoirs. Over 24,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore this region.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Central (Alicante)?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952587071/la-serra-gelada-circular-from-benidorm-via-altea-port-and-alfas-del-pi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serra Gelada – Circular from Benidorm via Altea Port and Alfás del Pi&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 23.3-mile route. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952607729/from-relleu-to-villajoyosa-circular-via-the-amadorio-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Relleu to Villajoyosa – Circular via the Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 24.1-mile ride.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Central (Alicante)?
A: The region offers a variety of natural features and landmarks. You can encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m)&lt;/a&gt;, as well as viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique geological experience, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt;, a cave formation.

Q: Are there any historical sites or man-made monuments accessible by touring bicycle?
A: Yes, touring cyclists can explore historical sites and man-made monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952433794/alicante-city-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante city tour&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through urban areas where you can access landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/607512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; along coastal routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Central (Alicante)?
A: The Alicante region, with its Mediterranean climate, is particularly pleasant for touring cycling during the fall, winter, and spring months (October to May). These seasons offer milder temperatures compared to the summer, making for more comfortable rides along the coast and through the inland hills.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region's diverse terrain includes routes that pass by reservoirs. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952607729/from-relleu-to-villajoyosa-circular-via-the-amadorio-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Relleu to Villajoyosa – Circular via the Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the Amadorio Reservoir.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain passes for touring cycling in Central (Alicante)?
A: Absolutely. The inland areas of Central (Alicante) feature mountainous terrain with challenging climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19454791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narrow Streets of Finestrat – Port de Tudons loop from Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31797684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Sella&lt;/a&gt; include significant elevation gains, with the latter ascending to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt;, a well-known mountain pass.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Central (Alicante) provide stunning panoramic views. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952029024/benidorm-the-cross-and-tossal-de-la-cala-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benidorm: The Cross and Tossal de la Cala Trail&lt;/a&gt; is known for offering panoramic viewpoints over Benidorm. Additionally, specific highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana&lt;/a&gt; are designated viewpoints along various routes.
tips: Very nice section of this route through a beautiful, quiet landscape! Brilliant views of the coast. It's worth it!
